modelInfo.name: LA City Hall (Base Isolated)
modelInfo.description: Los Angeles City Hall — a 32-story, 460 ft tall steel frame building, the tallest base-isolated structure in the world at time of retrofit (2001). Three structural segments with setbacks: podium (sub-basement to 2nd floor), midrise (3rd-9th), and tower (10th-32nd). Isolated with 416 high-damping rubber bearings and 90 flat sliding bearings, plus 64 viscous dampers. Retrofit designed by Nabih Youssef & Associates with AC Martin Partners.
